subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Core Components
- **1 atlas map** — A large fold-out map of the Wandering Sea divided into numbered regions. Players physically move their ship token across this map.
- **1 storybook** — A spiral-bound book with hundreds of numbered entries describing locations, encounters, and story events.
- **Ship board** — Tracks the Manticore's (your ship) condition, supplies, and crew positions.

#### Crew
- **9 crew cards** — Each crew member (including Captain Sofi Odessa) has unique abilities, health, and equipment slots. Crew members can be injured, healed, and equipped.
- **Crew ability tokens** — Track ability usage.

#### Cards and Tokens
- **Event cards** — Random events that occur during travel.
- **Challenge cards** — Drawn during skill checks to determine difficulty.
- **Equipment cards** — Weapons, armor, and tools that improve crew abilities.
- **Quest cards** — Ongoing objectives tracking story progress.
- **Totem tokens** — The 14 totems needed to return home. The primary campaign goal.
- **Level tokens** — Track crew experience and upgrades.
- **Supply tokens** — Food and resources for the ship.
- **Damage tokens** — Track injuries and ship damage.
- **Enemy standees** — Used during combat encounters.
- **Combat tokens** — Attack and defense markers.

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Map Setup
1. **Unfold the atlas** — Spread the large map on the table or a nearby surface.
2. **Place the ship** — Put the Manticore ship token on the starting location.
3. **Storybook** — Keep the storybook nearby but closed. You will reference specific numbered entries as you explore.

#### Ship Board Setup
1. **Crew placement** — Assign all 9 crew members to their positions on the ship board.
2. **Starting health** — Set all crew to full health.
3. **Starting supplies** — Place starting supply tokens on the ship.
4. **Command tokens** — Set the available command tokens for the turn.

#### Campaign vs. Single Session
- **Campaign** — The game is designed as a campaign spanning multiple sessions (total playtime 10-20 hours).
- **Save system** — The game includes a save/resume system. Bag all tokens and note your position, crew state, and quest progress.
- **Single session** — A single sitting plays 2-4 hours before saving.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Player Roles
- **Cooperative** — All players work together. There is no competitive element.
- **Crew division** — Each player controls a subset of the 9 crew members (divided evenly).
- **Solo play** — One player controls all crew members.

#### Starting State
- **No equipment** — Crew starts without equipment.
- **Quest log** — Begin with the introductory quest guiding you toward your first objectives.
- **Totems found** — Start with 0 of 14 totems.

#### How to Begin
- **Read the introduction** — The storybook entry for the starting location sets the narrative scene.
- **First destination** — Choose where to sail based on the introductory clues.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content:
#### Core Concept
Sleeping Gods is a cooperative campaign adventure game. Players control Captain Sofi Odessa and her crew, lost in a strange world aboard the steamship Manticore. You explore a vast atlas map, reading storybook entries at each location, making choices that shape the narrative, engaging in combat, and searching for 14 totems needed to return home. The game saves between sessions, creating an evolving campaign.

#### Game Arc
- **Campaign length** — 10-20+ hours across multiple sessions.
- **Open world** — No fixed path. You choose where to sail and what to investigate.
- **Totem hunt** — Find 14 totems hidden throughout the world. More totems = better ending.
- **Crew management** — Keep your crew healthy and equipped. If too many crew are incapacitated, the campaign can end poorly.
- **Multiple endings** — The number of totems found determines your ending quality.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
Each turn (representing a period of time in the world):

1. **Event phase** — Draw an event card. Events can be beneficial, harmful, or trigger story moments. Some events damage the ship or crew.
2. **Action phase** — The group takes 2 actions (chosen from the available options):
   - **Travel** — Move the ship to an adjacent region on the atlas map.
   - **Explore** — At your current location, read the corresponding storybook entry and follow its instructions.
   - **Market** — Visit a market (if available at your location) to buy/sell equipment and supplies.
   - **Rest** — Heal crew members. Remove damage tokens.
   - **Equip** — Assign or rearrange equipment among crew members.
3. **Upkeep phase** — Consume supplies (food). If you cannot feed the crew, they take damage.

#### Command Tokens
- **Spend command tokens** — During actions, players can spend command tokens to activate crew abilities, improve skill checks, or take bonus actions.
- **Recovery** — Command tokens refresh at the start of each turn.
- **Limited resource** — Managing command tokens is key to efficient turns.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Exploration
- **Storybook entries** — When you explore a location, look up the numbered entry in the storybook.
- **Narrative choices** — Entries often present choices (help or ignore, fight or negotiate, explore deeper or leave).
- **Consequences** — Choices have real consequences: gaining equipment, allies, totems, or suffering injuries and losses.
- **Keywords** — Some entries are only accessible if you have specific keywords (earned from previous choices).

#### Skill Checks
- **Challenge cards** — When the story calls for a skill check, draw challenge cards showing a target number.
- **Crew abilities** — Use crew members' stats + equipment bonuses to meet or exceed the target.
- **Success/failure** — The storybook describes different outcomes for success and failure.
- **Partial success** — Some checks have tiered outcomes.

#### Combat
- **Enemy encounters** — Some storybook entries lead to combat.
- **Grid-based** — Enemies are placed on a combat grid with hit zones.
- **Crew attacks** — Each crew member can attack using their weapon stats.
- **Targeting zones** — Enemies have different hit zones (head, body, legs, etc.) that you must target to deal damage.
- **Enemy attacks** — Enemies attack your crew based on patterns shown on their cards.
- **Damage** — Crew members can be injured (flipped face-down) and are less effective until healed.
- **Defeat** — If too many crew are injured, you may be forced to retreat.

#### Equipment
- **Weapons** — Swords, guns, bows improve attack capabilities.
- **Armor** — Reduces incoming damage.
- **Tools** — Provide bonuses to skill checks.
- **Equipment slots** — Each crew member has limited equipment slots.
- **Acquiring** — Found through exploration, markets, and quest rewards.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Crew Management
- **9 crew members** — Each has unique stats (strength, perception, diplomacy, stealth) and special abilities.
- **Health** — Each crew member has a health track. When health reaches 0, they are incapacitated.
- **Incapacitation** — Incapacitated crew cannot act. If too many are incapacitated simultaneously, the game may end.
- **Leveling up** — Crew gain experience and can be upgraded with improved stats and new abilities.

#### Ship Condition
- **Manticore damage** — The ship can take damage from events and encounters.
- **Repair** — Spend resources and actions to repair the ship.
- **Ship loss** — If the ship takes too much damage, the campaign can end.

#### Save/Resume System
- **Saving** — At the end of any session, record: ship position, crew health/equipment, quest progress, totems found, resources.
- **Resume** — Next session, restore the game state from the save.
- **Bags** — Use provided bags to store tokens between sessions.

#### Keywords and Flags
- **Keywords** — Earned through story choices. Keywords unlock specific storybook entries.
- **Tracking** — Write down keywords as you earn them.
- **Non-linear** — Keywords allow the story to branch based on your previous decisions.

#### Supplies
- **Food consumption** — Each turn costs supplies.
- **Running low** — Without supplies, crew take damage.
- **Resupply** — Markets and exploration can provide supplies.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Campaign End
The campaign ends when one of these occurs:
- **All 14 totems found** — Best possible ending.
- **Time runs out** — The event deck runs out, forcing the campaign to conclude.
- **Crew collapse** — Too many crew incapacitated simultaneously.
- **Voluntary end** — Players choose to attempt the final challenge with whatever totems they have.

#### Endings
- **Totem count** — The number of totems found determines the ending quality:
  - **0-4 totems** — Bad ending.
  - **5-8 totems** — Mediocre ending.
  - **9-12 totems** — Good ending.
  - **13-14 totems** — Best ending.
- **Story conclusion** — Read the corresponding ending entry in the storybook.

#### No Traditional Scoring
- **Cooperative** — There is no winner among players. The group succeeds or fails together.
- **Narrative satisfaction** — The reward is the story experienced and the ending achieved.
- **Replayability** — The open world means different paths can be explored in future campaigns.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Explore broadly early** — Visit many locations to gather clues, equipment, and keywords before focusing on totems.
- **Keep crew healthy** — An injured crew is an ineffective crew. Rest when needed rather than pushing through.
- **Manage supplies carefully** — Running out of food is devastating. Always know where your next resupply is.
- **Read carefully** — Storybook entries contain clues about where totems and important items might be found.
- **Equip wisely** — Give weapons to your best fighters and tools to your skill-check specialists.
- **Don't fight everything** — Some combats are optional. Retreat is sometimes the best option.
- **Take notes** — Write down interesting locations, NPCs, and clues for future reference.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
#### Exploration Strategy
- **Systematic coverage** — Work through regions methodically rather than jumping around randomly.
- **Follow quest threads** — Quests often lead to totems. Prioritize quest completion.
- **Revisit with keywords** — When you earn new keywords, revisit locations that previously had locked entries.

#### Combat Strategy
- **Target priority** — Focus fire on the most dangerous enemies first.
- **Use crew abilities** — Each crew member's special ability can turn the tide of combat.
- **Zone targeting** — Hit vulnerable zones on enemies for bonus damage.
- **Command tokens in combat** — Save command tokens for critical combat moments.

#### Resource Management
- **Supply buffer** — Always maintain at least 2-3 turns worth of supplies.
- **Market runs** — When near a market with excess equipment, sell unneeded items for supplies.
- **Heal efficiently** — Rest at locations that provide bonus healing when possible.

#### Campaign Pacing
- **Don't rush** — The campaign has limited time, but exploring thoroughly yields better results than speed-running.
- **Totem tracking** — Keep a running count and set targets for each session.
- **Prioritize as time dwindles** — As the event deck shrinks, focus on totem acquisition over side exploration.
